name: White Grape Jelly
recipeInstructions:
1. Tested February 1994 with half a teaspoon of liquid SugarTwin and whole cloves.
2. Sprinkle gelatin over one-fourth cup grape juice.
3. Combine the remaining grape juice, lemon juice, and allspice berries or whole cloves in a medium saucepan.
4. Boil vigorously for about seven minutes to reduce to three-quarters of a cup. (Test batch took approximately 15 minutes in a small pot.)
5. Remove from heat. Stir in sweetener and softened gelatin until it dissolves.
6. Discard allspice berries or cloves.
7. Pour into a sterilized jar. Cover tightly.
8. Store in the refrigerator.
2 teaspoons serving = 12 calories, 3 grams carbohydrate, 1++ Extra
Adapted from Choice Cooking, Canadian Diabetes Association 1986 Shared and tested by Elizabeth Rodier, February 1994
NOTE: If you want to make this with regular sugar, add it to the boiling juice.
Cinnamon Apple Jelly may be made with apple juice, one-inch piece cinnamon stick, one drop each yellow and red food coloring if desired, and four teaspoons artificial sweetener equivalent to sugar.
